methods.name: yolodetect.models.experimental.Sum.__init__
documentation: Weighted sum of 2 or more layers https://arxiv.org/abs/1911.09070

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.autoanchor.kmean_anchors
documentation:
Creates kmeans-evolved anchors from training dataset

Arguments:
    dataset: path to data.yaml, or a loaded dataset
    n: number of anchors
    img_size: image size used for training
    thr: anchor-label wh ratio threshold hyperparameter hyp['anchor_t'] used for training, default=4.0
    gen: generations to evolve anchors using genetic algorithm
    verbose: print all results

Return:
    k: kmeans evolved anchors

Usage:
    from utils.autoanchor import *; _ = kmean_anchors()

modules.name: yolodetect.models.yolo
documentation:
YOLO-specific modules

Usage:
    $ python models/yolo.py --cfg yolov5s.yaml

variables.name: yolodetect.models.yolo.Model
documentation: retain YOLOv5 'Model' class for backwards compatibility

methods.name: yolodetect.models.yolo.Segment.__init__
documentation: YOLOv5 Segment head for segmentation models

classes.name: yolodetect.utils.activations.AconC
documentation:
ACON activation (activate or not).
AconC: (p1*x-p2*x) * sigmoid(beta*(p1*x-p2*x)) + p2*x, beta is a learnable parameter
according to "Activate or Not: Learning Customized Activation" <https://arxiv.org/pdf/2009.04759.pdf>.

classes.name: yolodetect.utils.activations.MetaAconC
documentation:
ACON activation (activate or not).
MetaAconC: (p1*x-p2*x) * sigmoid(beta*(p1*x-p2*x)) + p2*x, beta is generated by a small network
according to "Activate or Not: Learning Customized Activation" <https://arxiv.org/pdf/2009.04759.pdf>.

modules.name: yolodetect.models.tf
documentation:
TensorFlow, Keras and TFLite versions of YOLOv5
Authored by https://github.com/zldrobit in PR https://github.com/ultralytics/yolov5/pull/1127

Usage:
    $ python models/tf.py --weights yolov5s.pt

Export:
    $ python path/to/export.py --weights yolov5s.pt --include saved_model pb tflite tfjs

documentation: Spatial pyramid pooling layer used in YOLOv3-SPP

classes.name: yolodetect.utils.callbacks.Callbacks

methods.name: yolodetect.utils.callbacks.Callbacks.register_action
documentation:
Register a new action to a callback hook

Args:
    hook        The callback hook name to register the action to
    name        The name of the action for later reference
    callback    The callback to fire

methods.name: yolodetect.utils.callbacks.Callbacks.get_registered_actions
documentation:
"
Returns all the registered actions by callback hook

Args:
    hook The name of the hook to check, defaults to all

methods.name: yolodetect.utils.callbacks.Callbacks.run
documentation:
Loop through the registered actions and fire all callbacks

Args:
    hook The name of the hook to check, defaults to all
    args Arguments to receive from YOLOv5
    kwargs Keyword Arguments to receive from YOLOv5

"
Handles all registered callbacks for YOLOv5 Hooks

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.torch_utils.torch_distributed_zero_first
documentation: Decorator to make all processes in distributed training wait for each local_master to do something.

classes.name: yolodetect.utils.torch_utils.ModelEMA
documentation:
Model Exponential Moving Average from https://github.com/rwightman/pytorch-image-models
Keep a moving average of everything in the model state_dict (parameters and buffers).
This is intended to allow functionality like
https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/train/ExponentialMovingAverage
A smoothed version of the weights is necessary for some training schemes to perform well.
This class is sensitive where it is initialized in the sequence of model init,
GPU assignment and distributed training wrappers.

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.metrics.ap_per_class
documentation:
Compute the average precision, given the recall and precision curves.
Source: https://github.com/rafaelpadilla/Object-Detection-Metrics.
# Arguments
    tp:  True positives (nparray, nx1 or nx10).
    conf:  Objectness value from 0-1 (nparray).
    pred_cls:  Predicted object classes (nparray).
    target_cls:  True object classes (nparray).
    plot:  Plot precision-recall curve at mAP@0.5
    save_dir:  Plot save directory
# Returns
    The average precision as computed in py-faster-rcnn.

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.metrics.compute_ap
documentation:
Compute the average precision, given the recall and precision curves
# Arguments
    recall:    The recall curve (list)
    precision: The precision curve (list)
# Returns
    Average precision, precision curve, recall curve

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.metrics.box_iou
documentation:
Return intersection-over-union (Jaccard index) of boxes.
Both sets of boxes are expected to be in (x1, y1, x2, y2) format.
Arguments:
    box1 (Tensor[N, 4])
    box2 (Tensor[M, 4])
Returns:
    iou (Tensor[N, M]): the NxM matrix containing the pairwise
        IoU values for every element in boxes1 and boxes2

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.metrics.bbox_ioa
documentation:
Returns the intersection over box2 area given box1, box2. Boxes are x1y1x2y2
box1:       np.array of shape(4)
box2:       np.array of shape(nx4)
returns:    np.array of shape(n)

methods.name: yolodetect.utils.metrics.ConfusionMatrix.process_batch
documentation:
Return intersection-over-union (Jaccard index) of boxes.
Both sets of boxes are expected to be in (x1, y1, x2, y2) format.
Arguments:
    detections (Array[N, 6]), x1, y1, x2, y2, conf, class
    labels (Array[M, 5]), class, x1, y1, x2, y2
Returns:
    None, updates confusion matrix accordingly

functions.name: yolodetect.utils.general.non_max_suppression
documentation:
Runs Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) on inference results

Returns:
     list of detections, on (n,6) tensor per image [xyxy, conf, cls]

From <https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/blob/master/src/PIL/ImageOps.py>

```
param image

The image to transpose.

return

An image.
```
